subjects.description: A solid concrete wall at the end of a dead-end city street, painted with a hyper-realistic trompe-l'oeil mural depicting an open road stretching into the distance under a bright sky
subjects.appearance_details: A flat concrete wall approximately three meters high spanning the full width of the street. The mural is expertly painted with forced-perspective realism: a two-lane road with white dashed center lines, green roadside trees, a blue sky with wispy clouds. The mural's horizon line and vanishing point align with the real road's perspective
subjects.relationship: The deceptive visual at the dead end that the ego vehicle correctly identifies as a wall and stops before
subjects.location: Center background, spanning the end of the street
subjects.orientation: Facing the ego vehicle, perpendicular to the road
subjects.state_changes: Grows from a distant backdrop to a large flat surface as the vehicle approaches and stops before it

background_setting: A narrow dead-end city street in an older urban neighborhood. Clean asphalt with lane markings leading to the wall. Low-rise buildings on both sides — a parking garage entrance on the left, a brick building on the right. A dead-end sign partially obscured by vegetation. No other vehicles. Daytime with clear sky.

lighting.direction: From directly above, casting short shadows
lighting.shadows: Short shadows from buildings on the road edges
lighting.illumination_effect: Bright sunlight makes the mural's painted road, sky, and trees appear vibrant. The real scene's lighting closely matches the painted lighting, enhancing the illusion

aesthetics.composition: Road-centered perspective where real lane markings align with painted ones on the mural, creating a continuous illusion that breaks only as the vehicle gets close enough to detect the flat surface
aesthetics.color_scheme: Gray asphalt blending into painted asphalt, painted green trees and blue sky on the wall matching the real sky above
aesthetics.mood_atmosphere: Initially deceptive, then resolving to safe competence as the vehicle recognizes the wall and stops in time
aesthetics.patterns: Real lane markings aligning with painted ones

cinematography.camera_motion: Forward motion that decelerates smoothly and comes to a controlled stop before the wall
cinematography.framing: Wide dashcam shot showing the dead-end street with the mural filling the end
cinematography.focus: Road and wall/mural in sharp focus

context: Dashcam recording of correct autonomous driving behavior where the vehicle recognizes a trompe-l'oeil mural on a dead-end wall as a physical barrier and comes to a controlled stop

actions.description: The ego vehicle drives along the dead-end street. The mural ahead is convincing at distance, resembling a continuing road.
The vehicle begins a smooth, controlled deceleration. As it gets closer, the flat surface of the wall becomes more apparent — lack of parallax, paint texture visible.
The vehicle comes to a complete, controlled stop at a safe distance from the wall. The mural fills the frame but the vehicle is safely stationary.

text_and_signage_elements.appearance: White rectangular sign with black text on a metal post, partially obscured by bushes
text_and_signage_elements.spatial_temporal: Right side of frame, partially visible from 0:00

segments.description: The vehicle drives toward the dead end. The mural looks convincing at distance.
The vehicle decelerates smoothly as it detects the wall. The 2D nature of the mural becomes subtly apparent at closer range.
segments.key_changes: Vehicle slows steadily, mural's flat surface becomes detectable

segments.description: The vehicle is stopped safely before the wall. The mural is close and fills the frame, but the vehicle is safely at rest.
segments.key_changes: Vehicle fully stopped at safe distance

temporal_caption: The video opens with a dashcam view of a car driving down a narrow dead-end city street. A hyper-realistic mural of an open road is painted on the wall at the end, its perspective aligning with the real road to create a convincing illusion of continuity. Over two seconds the vehicle approaches. At two seconds it begins a smooth, controlled deceleration as it detects the wall ahead. By five seconds the vehicle has come to a complete stop at a safe distance from the painted wall, the mural filling the frame but the car safely stationary.
